articles.title: A Breakdown Of The DOJ’s Immigration Departures
articles.content:
编辑说明：欢迎来到我们的每日栏目每日趣味问答！

根据彭博法律（Bloomberg Law）的一份报道，司法部移民诉讼办公室自2025年1月以来，至少有多少名律师辞职、退休或以其他方式离开该办公室？

提示：尽管人才大量流失，司法部一位发言人表示，移民诉讼办公室“正继续全力应对 defended immigration cases，并对那些滥用美国公民身份的人提起剥夺国籍案件。”

请见下一页答案。

本文司法部移民部门离职情况分析首次发布于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
Ed. Note: Welcome to our daily feature Trivia Question of the Day!

According to a report by Bloomberg Law, the Department of Justice’s Office of Immigration Litigation has seen at lest how many attorneys quit, retire, or otherwise leave the office since January 2025?

Hint: Despite the brain drain, a DOJ spokesperson said the Office of Immigration Litigation is “continuing to fire on all cylinders to defend immigration cases and file denaturalization cases against those who take advantage of American citizenship.”

See the answer on the next page.

The post A Breakdown Of The DOJ’s Immigration Departures appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Back To Basics
articles.content:
在最近的法律科技连接律师事务所研究创新大会上，格雷格·兰伯特向法律研究界提出了一个问题：有多少与会者遇到过不知道Westlaw或Lexis密码的合伙人？这个问题在整个会场引起了共鸣，大多数人都有过这样的经历。

众所周知，合伙人很少亲自进行法律研究，但如果他们不知道如何登录法律研究平台，那么事务所使用什么流程来让合伙人在简报中获取原始资料？

在过去一年里，我们看到了一系列关于AI幻觉的新闻标题，虚假案例、伪造的引用和错误陈述的事实在审查过程中蒙混过关，最终出现在法庭文件中。

技术不是问题所在——这是一个流程问题

虽然律师对自己的工作负责，但有一种倾向是将问题归咎于在流程中引入AI技术。我认为更好的解释是，AI暴露了大多数流程中已经存在的缺陷。

几十年来，律师事务所的起草工作建立在合伙人的指导和associate写作之上，由高级associate先行审阅，然后合伙人以监督角色进行审查。在此过程中，associate会使用Shepard's或KeyCite等引文工具。但在实践中，监督律师在何时、如何以及以何种方式审查工作？

如果在流程早期已经审查过某个案例，在流程结束时是否还会再次检查，包括从该案例中引用的内容？

在AI出现之前，在流程缝隙中漏掉的错误并不那么重要，因为潜在错误很小，必要时可以作为勘误表更正。但现在这种方法已经行不通了。人类创作正在被AI生成的内容和编辑所增强，这些内容可以改变事实、伪造引用，甚至编造不存在的案例引文。

AI暴露了一直存在的漏洞

任何经常使用AI语法检查软件或微软Copilot的人都经历过这样的体验：AI的修改虽然语法更正确，但也改变了语句的含义。Associate们仍在学习，可能会忽略这些强大新工具引入的微妙错误。

在文档早期阶段正确的内容，很可能在后期版本中被AI改变。

勘误表与伪造

核心问题在于，AI幻觉是一种伪造。虚假案例是一个问题，但最近文件中出现的问题还包括伪造的引用。在提交前，需要对所有内容（包括基础事实）进行完整审查。

曾经假设人类创作的久经考验的工作流程必须改变。

起草和审查不可分离

还记得高中科学课吗？原子内有质子、中子和电子，但原子本身不可分割。AI在文档创建中凸显了这一点。起草和审查流程是原子性的。它们不能分离，否则会产生某种核反应。

AI在起草中提高了效率，但也给审查流程带来了新的负担，并揭示了关于谁在何时审查什么的假设。

现在流程需要追踪谁验证引文、何时验证以及如何在起草和审查文档的团队之间沟通。同样需要追踪引用和事实核查。

零信任原则

在网络安全领域，有一个概念叫做零信任。基本上就是“永不信任，始终验证”。不幸的是，AI系统是概率性的。它们实际上是在猜测下一个符合模式的单词。这意味着幻觉是系统设计的特性，而不是缺陷。有技术可以减少幻觉，但无法消除。

“回归基础”审查流程

将AI融入起草的后果是，错误可能变成远超勘误表范围的伪造。

在互联网出现之前，将所有权威资料打印出来并装订成册以供查阅和审查是很常见的。但这种物理装订本的数字等价物在某些事务所已经失传。

有现成的解决方案可以帮助组织和改进流程。在文档中创建到权威资料库的超链接可以帮助加快审查。如果某个案例的链接不存在，那就是一个红旗，表明该案例可能不存在。点击链接也可以帮助审查和验证引用或事实是否正确。

事务所现在应该采取行动

AI政策不是流程。事务所需要将律师如何遵守政策具体化，并提供使遵守规则11道德责任标准更容易的流程和工具。

加州律师协会最近就专业行为规则的修改征求了意见，这些修改涉及AI。有人提议制定一个实际的超链接规则，以确保在文件中链接资料来源。事务所不应等待监管机构告诉他们该怎么做。

结论

AI可能将严重伪造引入了法律工作，但也消除了审查流程曾经密不透风的假象。

有更先进的解决方案是可能的，包括我在之前关于幻觉的文章中提出的一些建议。但在行业考虑更先进的解决方案之前，应该首先确保解决基本问题。

那些认识到这一点并重建审查流程、将其与起草流程紧密耦合的事务所，将比那些将此视为技术问题或仅通过政策就能解决的问题的事务所处于更好的位置。

本文的审查使用了AI。

肯·克拉奇菲尔德在法律、税务和其他行业拥有超过四十年的经验。在他的职业生涯中，他专注于增长、创新和业务转型。他的咨询业务为投资者、法律科技初创公司和其他客户提供建议。作为一个了解市场并善于创造满足客户需求产品的战略思想家，他曾在初创公司和大型企业工作。他曾在六家企业担任综合管理职务。肯对影响市场的趋势有敏锐的洞察力。无论是1980年代的互联网还是生成式AI，他都理解技术及其如何影响业务。克拉奇菲尔德的职业生涯始于LexisNexis的实习生，此后曾在汤森路透社、彭博社、邓白氏和威科集团工作。肯拥有MBA学位和俄亥俄州立大学电气工程学士学位。
articles.summary:
Something jumped out at me early at the recent LegalTech Connect Law Firm Research & Innovation Conference with the legal research community. Greg Lambert asked how many attendees have encountered partners who don’t know their Westlaw or Lexis passwords. That question resonated throughout the room as most had experienced this. 

It’s general knowledge that partners rarely conduct legal research, but if they don’t know how to log in to a legal research platform, what process are firms using for partners to access the underlying sources in a brief?  

Over the past year, we’ve seen a steady stream of headlines about AI hallucinations where fake cases, fabricated quotes, and misstated facts made it through a review process and into court filings.

Technology Isn’t The Issue; It’s A Process Problem

While lawyers are responsible for their work, there is a tendency to blame the breakdown on the introduction of AI technology into the process. I think a better explanation is that AI exposed a flaw that already existed in most processes.

For decades, law firm drafting was built on partner guidance and associate writing, with a senior associate taking a pass and then the partner reviewing in a supervisory role. Citation tools such as Shepard’s or KeyCite are used by the associate along the way. But in practice, how, what, and when does the supervising attorney review the work? 

If a case was reviewed earlier in the process, is it checked again at the end of the process, including the quotation pulled from the case? 

Before AI, a mistake that squeaked through a gap in the process didn’t matter as much because potential errors were minor and could be corrected as errata if necessary. But that doesn’t work anymore. Human authorship is being augmented by AI-generated content and editing that can alter facts, fabricate quotes, and invent citations to nonexistent cases. 

AI Exposes The Gaps That Were Always There

Anyone who uses AI grammar-checking software or Microsoft Copilot on a regular basis has had the experience of “uncorrecting” its revisions that are more grammatically correct but also change the meaning of a statement. Associates are still learning and may miss subtle errors introduced by these powerful new tools.

What was correct at an earlier stage of a document may very well be changed by AI in a later version. 

Errata Versus Fabrication

The central issue here is that an AI hallucination is a fabrication. Fake cases are one issue, but more recent issues in filings include fake quotations.  A complete review of everything, including foundational facts, needs to be conducted at the end of the process before filing.  

The tried-and-true workflows that once assumed human authorship must change. 

Drafting And Review Should Be Inseparable

Remember your high school science class? There are protons, neutrons, and electrons within an atom, but the atom itself cannot be separated. AI highlights this in document creation.  The drafting and review processes are atomic.  They can’t be separated without some kind of nuclear reaction.  

AI creates efficiencies in drafting, but it imposes new burdens on the review process and highlights assumptions about who reviews what and when. 

The process now needs to track who verifies citations, when verification occurs, and how it is communicated across the team drafting and reviewing the document.  The same should happen for quotations and fact-checking.  

Zero Trust principles

In Cybersecurity, there is a concept called Zero Trust.  It’s basically “never trust, always verify”.  Unfortunately, AI systems are probabilistic.  They are literally guessing the next part of a word that fits a pattern. This means that hallucinations are a feature of the system’s design rather than a bug. There are techniques to minimize hallucinations, but they can’t be eliminated. 

“Back to Basics” review processes

The consequences of AI incorporated into drafting are that errors can become fabrications that are well beyond the scope of errata.

Before the Internet, it was common to print out all authorities and keep them in binders for access and review.   But the digital equivalent to that physical binder has been lost in some firms. 

There are solutions available to help organize and improve processes. Creating hyperlinks within documents to a repository of referenced authoritative sources can help expedite review. If a link doesn’t exist to a case, then it’s a red flag that it may not exist. Clicking on a link can help review and verify that quotes or facts are correct, too.

Firms should take action now

AI Policy is not a process.  Firms need to operationalize how attorneys can comply with the policy and provide the processes and tools needed to make it easier to meet the standards of Rule 11 Ethical responsibility. 

The California Bar recently solicited comments on changes to the rules of professional conduct as they pertain to AI.  And there is some discussion proposing an actual Hyperlink Rule to ensure sources are linked in filings.  Firms shouldn’t wait for regulators to tell them what to do. 

Conclusion

AI may have introduced gross fabrication into legal work, but it also removed the illusion that review processes were ever airtight.

There are more advanced solutions possible, including some I have suggested in prior writings on hallucinations. But before the industry considers more advanced solutions, it should ensure it addresses the basics first. 

The firms that recognize and rebuild their review processes and tightly couple them to the drafting process will be in a much better position than those that treat this as a problem caused by technology or a problem to be solved with policy alone.

AI was used in the review of this article.

Ken Crutchfield has over forty years of experience in legal, tax, and other industries. Throughout his career, he has focused on growth, innovation, and business transformation. His consulting practice advises investors, legal tech startups and others. As a strategic thinker who understands markets and creating products to meet customer needs, he has worked in start-ups and large enterprises. He has served in General Management capacities in six businesses. Ken has a pulse on the trends affecting the market. Whether it was the Internet in the 1980s or Generative AI, he understands technology and how it can impact business. Crutchfield started his career as an intern with LexisNexis and has worked at Thomson Reuters, Bloomberg, Dun & Bradstreet, and Wolters Kluwer. Ken has an MBA and holds a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from The Ohio State University.

The post Back To Basics appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: Considerations When Withdrawing As Counsel For Nonpayment Of Fees
articles.content:
一些律师比较幸运，很少或几乎从未遇到过不支付账单的客户。然而，大多数律师都不可避免地会遇到某个未付款的客户，而且该客户将来似乎也不太可能付款。如果律师为当事人处理的案件涉及诉讼，那么律师可能需要征得法庭许可才能退出代理，这一过程可能很耗时。在决定是否应因未付款而退出代理时，律师应记住几件事。

我认识的一些律师在客户错过付款截止日期后不久就选择退出代理。然而，稍作等待可能是值得的。客户有一种非凡的能力，即使在数月未付款后仍能全额支付账款。我见过客户在发票开出近一年后才支付大额账单的情况。退出代理可能会破坏律师与客户的关系，因此给予客户一些善意可能很重要。

此外，法院可能不希望律师在错过付款截止日期后不久就想退出与客户的代理关系。通常，时间越长，律师可以出示的被忽略付款通知越多，法院越可能允许律师退出代理。此外，如果客户最终支付了这些账单，退出可能涉及一些可以避免的工作和费用。

决定是否退出的另一个重要因素是诉讼所处的阶段。律师通常不应等到案件准备开庭时才退出。因为法院可能会认为，如果在案件后期退出代理，将损害客户和其他利益相关方的利益。如果开庭日期临近，这可能有利于提前向法院申请退出许可，而不是等到最后才申请，以最大限度地提高法院批准退出申请的机会。

在请求退出代理时，与案件中其他当事人的代理律师建立良好关系也很有帮助。客户很少反对退出代理的动词。但我见过其他律师对退出代理提出异议的情况。有时律师不希望案件暂停以便当事人获得新的代理律师，有时律师认为当事人的利益最好由一直处理该案件的律师来代理。然而，我亲眼见过律师因专业礼让而不反对退出动词的情况，这也是保持良好职业关系的另一个原因。

在请求退出代理时，通知客户将要进行的程序也很重要。通常，法院要求律师向客户送达与退出请求相关的命令和动议文件，这对客户来说可能很困惑。事实上，我曾遇到一位客户，他以为自己被起诉了，因为退出动议的相关文件被送达到他家中。与客户沟通可以帮助更轻松地送达必要文件，并保持客户对代理律师的一些善意。

总的来说，在因未付款而请求法院退出代理之前，通常给客户至少几个月的时间来付款是合理的。然而，诉讼的状态可能会决定律师何时提交退出动议。

乔丹·罗斯曼是罗斯曼律师事务所的合伙人，这是一家位于纽约和新泽明州的综合性律师事务所。他还是“学生债务日记”网站的创始人，该网站讨论了他如何还清学生贷款。您可以通过电子邮件jordan@rothman.law与乔丹联系。

本文《因费用未付而退出代理的考虑因素》首次发表于Above the Law网站。
articles.summary:
Some lawyers are lucky and rarely, if ever, need to deal with clients who do not pay their bills. However, the vast amount of attorneys will invariably face a client that has not paid their bills and seems unlikely to do so in the future. If the matter a lawyer is handling for the client involves litigation, the attorney may need to ask the court permission to withdraw as counsel, which can be a time-consuming process. There are a few things lawyers should keep in mind when deciding if they should withdraw as counsel for nonpayment.

I know some lawyers who withdraw as counsel shortly after a missed payment deadline. However, it might be worthwhile to wait a little before doing so. Clients have an unusual ability to pay in full even after months of nonpayment have passed. I have seen clients pay substantial bills close to a year after invoices were first generated. Withdrawing as counsel might ruin an attorney-client relationship, so it might be important to extend the client a little good faith.

Moreover, a court might not appreciate that a lawyer wants to cut ties with a client shortly after a missed payment deadline. Generally, the more time that has passed, and the more ignored payment notices a lawyer can show were served, the more likely a court will be to permit counsel to withdraw. Moreover, withdrawing might involve work and expenses that can be avoided if the client eventually pays those invoices.

Another important factor in the decision to withdraw is the stage of the litigation. Lawyers generally should not wait until a case is trial-ready to withdraw from a matter. This is because courts might find that clients and other stakeholders may be prejudiced by a withdrawal of counsel too late in a case. If a trial date is approaching, this might militate in favor of asking for permission to withdraw earlier rather than later in order to maximize the chance withdrawal will be approved by the court.

It also pays to make friends with counsel for other parties to a case when asking permission to withdraw. Clients rarely oppose motions to withdraw as counsel, but I have seen other lawyers to a case file opposition to the withdrawal of counsel. Sometimes lawyers do not want a case stayed to allow a party to obtain new counsel, and other times, lawyers think their clients’ interests would be best served if a party is represented by counsel who has been handling the matter. However, I have seen firsthand lawyers not opposing withdrawal motions out of professional courtesy when counsel liked each other, so this is another reason to maintain solid professional relationships.

It is also important to keep clients apprised of the process that will be undertaken to permit counsel to withdraw. Often, courts require lawyers to serve clients with orders and motion papers related to the request to be relieved as counsel, and this can be confusing to a client. Indeed, I had one client who thought he was being sued since papers related to the motion to withdraw were served at his home.  Communication with clients can help make service of the necessary documents easier and preserve some goodwill the client may have for their counsel.

All told, it usually makes sense to give a client at least a few months to make payments before asking the court to withdraw as counsel for nonpayment. However, the state of the litigation may dictate when a lawyer files a motion to withdraw.

Jordan Rothman is a partner of The Rothman Law Firm, a full-service New York and New Jersey law firm. He is also the founder of Student Debt Diaries, a website discussing how he paid off his student loans. You can reach Jordan through email at jordan@rothman.law.

The post Considerations When Withdrawing As Counsel For Nonpayment Of Fees appeared first on Above the Law.

articles.title: DLA Piper Partner Backs Trump Central Park 5 Defamation Case
articles.content:
当你利用美国总统的职位大捞了一笔不义之财后，被当场抓到损害无辜者的名誉时，唯一该做的事就是道歉？才不是，那是穷人才做的事！当压力来临时，你要反击，花大价钱请最好的律师！路透社有报道：

美国总统特朗普在中央公园五人案定罪被推翻的诽谤诉讼中加强了辩护团队，聘请了知名律所DLA Piper的一位高级律师。

Caryn Schechtman是DLA Piper证券与金融调查业务的负责人，她签署了特朗普周四向第三巡回上诉法院提交的一份简报，要求推翻一项允许案件继续推进的裁决。

为特朗普工作是很大胆的举动。即使你忽略为一名被定罪的罪犯辩护的伦理复杂性——此人在被证清白后仍坚称那些人是强奸犯——回报会是什么？虽然特朗普很会赚钱，但他有大量记录显示他不付钱给为他工作的人。他已经坑了数百人。虽然你可能天真地认为他只会坑普通百姓而不会坑在法庭上救他命的律师，但他欠12家不同律所160万美元的事实表明恰恰相反。

话虽如此，他也有其他方式的“回报”。他照顾过Todd Blanche和Emil Bove，也许这正是Schechtman转型的新途径？与准独裁者打交道是在艰难时期确保工作安全的一种方式。如果不是这样，我们会很乐意把Schechtman加入被这位以坑律师著称的家伙坑过的律师名单。

【路透社】特朗普在中央公园五人诽谤案中扩大法律团队

此前：不经意的谎言让特朗普再次陷入法律麻烦

Chris Williams于2021年6月成为Above the Law的社交媒体经理和副编辑。在加入团队之前，他在Facebook群组"Law School Memes for Edgy T14s"中兼职担任小有名气的表情包大师。他忍受了密苏里州足够长的时间，毕业于圣路易斯华盛顿大学法学院。他曾是一名造船工人，现在正在学游泳，对修辞学、斯宾诺莎主义和幽默感兴趣。重新开始骑自行车也无妨。你可以通过christopherrashadwilliams@gmail.com联系他，也可以在Twitter/Bluesky上@WritesForRent。

这篇文章首先发表于Above the Law。
articles.summary:
When you’ve finessed an ungodly amount of money from leveraging your position as President of the United States, there’s only one thing to do when you’re caught red-handed tarnishing the legacy of innocent men. Apologize? No, that’s for poors! When push comes to shove, you shove back by spending money on some of the best lawyers you can find! Reuters has coverage:

U.S. President Donald Trump has beefed up his defense team in a defamation lawsuit related to the overturned Central Park Five convictions, adding a senior lawyer from prominent law firm DLA Piper.

Caryn ​Schechtman, who leads DLA Piper’s securities and financial investigations practice, signed a brief Trump filed Thursday in the ‌3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals seeking to overturn a decision that allowed the case to move forward.

Working for Trump is a bold move. Even if you brush aside the ethical complexities of defending a convicted felon who has extensive connections with the highest profile child sex ring in recent history after he accused exonerated men of being rapists, what’s the payoff gonna be? While Trump is good at getting paid, he has a heavily documented history of not paying the people who work for him. Dude has skimped out on hundreds of people. And as hopeful as it might be to think that he just screws over the little people and not the lawyers saving his ass in court, the $1.6M he owes to 12 different firms points to the contrary.

That said, he’s been known to pay in other ways. He’s taken care of Todd Blanche and Emil Bove, maybe this is Schechtman’s way of pivoting to a new role? Dealing with proto-dictators is one way to ensure job security in these trying times. And if it isn’t, we’ll be happy to add Schechtman to the list of lawyers that got screwed over by the guy known for screwing his lawyers over.

Trump Expands Legal Team With DLA Piper In Central Park Five Defamation Case [Reuters]
